_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to find experiences in stories by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander authors that are like our own experiences.

_source.w: We are learning to find experiences in stories by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander authors that are like our own experiences.
y: Connecting stories to our own lives helps us understand them more deeply.
Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander stories often share experiences that are familiar to all of us — like family, friendship and belonging.
Finding connections between stories and our lives helps us feel that stories are for us.
eg: We listen to a story by an Aboriginal author and talk about something similar that happened to us.
We watch a story by a Torres Strait Islander storyteller and find a character who reminds us of someone we know.
We compare something from a First Nations story with something from our own family life.
We draw a picture of a moment in the story that is like something we have experienced.
We discuss how themes like family, friendship and caring for Country connect to our own lives.
We share with a partner one way the story is like our life and one way it is different.
